mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in unemployment rate above 0.5 percentage points over 3 months signals deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production and rising inflation signal stagflationary pressures that depress equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in co-located INDPRO/CPIAUCSL declines are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening cycles characterized by FEDFUNDS above 4.5% generate negative S&P 500 forward returns through higher discount rates and reduced corporate borrowing capacity over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in GDP (GDPC1) indicates strong economic demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through improved corporate earnings expectations.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in GDP growth periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1500 thousand units signals weak consumer demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals a strong labor market, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through increased consumer spending.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in co-located PAYEMS/UNRATE improvements are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates expanding supply capacity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through improved corporate margins and economic optimism.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in INDPRO growth periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).
